paper_title: A simplified palaeoceanography archiving system (PARIS) and GUI for storage and visualisation of marine sediment core proxy data vs age and depth.

methods.description: Process for storing isotope and other palaeoclimate proxy data in the PARIS system against core depth, allowing for future application of new geochronologies and comparison of proxy data vs multiple geochronologies.

steps.description: Store isotope and other palaeoclimate proxy data against core depth as the primary format.
steps.searchable_summary: store proxy data against core depth

steps.description: Assign two depth values (depth1 and depth2) to each data point to represent the top and bottom of a core interval.
steps.searchable_summary: assign depth1 and depth2 to data points

steps.description: Use NaN for depth2 if not provided, assuming depth2 is 1 cm greater than depth1.
Structure data in a tab-delimited ASCII text format with specified column numbers for depth, measurement value, and measurement uncertainty.
Enter NaN as a placeholder for missing values to ensure structural integrity and machine-readability.

methods.description: Management of age-depth model files within the PARIS system to assign age and age uncertainty to raw data stored against depth.

steps.description: Store age-depth model files in a 'master' folder within the 'age models' directory.
steps.searchable_summary: store age-depth model files in master folder

steps.description: Allow different age model scenarios to be stored and accessed from the GUI.
steps.searchable_summary: enable storage of multiple age model scenarios

steps.description: Use the 'Undatable' output file format by default, with the option to adjust to other formats by editing the admodelformat.m file.
steps.searchable_summary: use and adjust age-depth model file formats
